finding: A completed trailing batch where every tool produced NO result at all sets batch_has_result=false and emits a bare Assistant tool_use (dangling), indistinguishable from awaiting-approval.
resolution: rejected-unreachable: the MCP layer persists a real OR is_error tool_result for every executed tool (all failure branches in mcp.rs), so a completed batch always has >=1 result; the zero-result case is ONLY awaiting-approval, whose result is appended separately. Docstring softened to state this exactly rather than overclaiming 'ALWAYS'.

finding: Docstring claimed the invariant is 'ALWAYS upheld' but the zero-result trailing batch is an exception.
resolution: fixed: docstring rewritten to scope the guarantee to batches whose tools have run and to explicitly document the awaiting-approval exception.

finding: The orphan-drop test only covers trailing orphans (which pre-fix also dropped); the behavior that actually changed — a mid-stream orphan [use A, result X, result A] that pre-fix emitted inside an invalid Tool turn — was untested.
Duplicate-result dedup (entry().or_insert first-wins) and duplicate tool_use ids were untested.
resolution: fixed dedup: added TEST-8 group_assistant_blocks_dedups_duplicate_result. Duplicate tool_use ids: rejected-out-of-scope (malformed input the function does not create; providers reject dup ids upstream).

finding: A mixed batch [use A(builtin, executed), use B(third-party, awaiting approval), result A] makes batch_has_result=true, synthesizing an is_error for the pending B.
resolution: accepted-correct: the re-audit confirmed this IS reachable (a builtin tool executes+persists while a third-party awaits approval), but the synthesized is_error for B is a VALID, TRANSIENT provider payload — never persisted, and the NEXT request re-assembles from fresh DB state after B's real result is appended, so there is no duplicate tool_result and no cross-request inconsistency. Strictly better than the pre-fix dangling tool_use.

finding: Duplicate tool_use ids [use A, use A, result A] emit two tool_use blocks id A plus real+synth results, which providers reject as duplicate tool_use_id.
resolution: rejected-out-of-scope: unique-id malformed input we neither create nor are asked to repair; a pre-existing non-guarantee independent of this fix.

finding: group_assistant_blocks and apply_summary_block are pure and synchronous over owned/locally-borrowed data with no shared mutable state.

The synthesized ToolResult converts correctly for all three adapters: Anthropic pairs by tool_use_id (is_error passed), OpenAI by tool_call_id, Gemini functionResponse by the carried name (no unknown_function fallback).
resolution: no action (positive confirmation the fix does not regress OpenAI/Gemini).

finding: Two parallel tool_uses sharing a function name yield two identically-named functionResponse parts that Gemini pairs ambiguously (by name, no id).
resolution: rejected-out-of-scope: pre-existing Gemini-adapter limitation that affects real results identically; not introduced by this fix.

finding: The forward-snap past leading Role::Tool is provider-safe: a retained-leading Tool is always an orphan whose Assistant tool_use was the immediately-preceding dropped message; loop stops at the first non-Tool and keeps drop_until >= system_prefix_len.
Helper placement, doc comments, HashMap first-wins/orphan-drop semantics, match style, and Role emission follow module idioms; no duplicate test-helper collisions.
No unwrap/expect/indexing panics: HashMap::remove has an unwrap_or_else fallback, apply_summary_block guards every index and clamps via min(len), and the placeholder text leaks no id/secret.
Synthesized text is a fixed constant; the only propagated field is the tool_use name already visible to the provider — no secret/host-path/internal-id leak.
Dropping orphan tool_results touches only the outbound array; stored DB history and mcp_tool_calls are untouched, so no downstream consumer loses data.
results_by_id is one tiny per-message HashMap; flush is O(k) and the trailing any() runs once — O(n) with cheap clones, no regression vs the old flat Vec.
The snap while-loop advances only over consecutive leading Role::Tool and is bounded by messages.len().
A synthesized is_error placeholder has no backing stored result, so if clear_old_tool_results later rewrites it to a get_tool_result(id) pointer, recall returns empty.
resolution: accepted-benign: the result was already absent (that is WHY it was synthesized), so get_tool_result returning not-found is consistent; the model learns the result is unavailable either way. No correctness impact; not worth special-casing.

finding: After the snap, drop_until stays within [system_prefix_len, len]; drain+insert leaves a consistent [System*, Summary, RecentTurns].
resolution: no action (clean). Proactively added a `drop_until > system_prefix_len` guard so a message_count=0 (drop-nothing) case never snaps a leading Tool.
